entry.title: Don't be afraid of (introducing) concepts
entry.summary:
During my last 3-4 years of my professional life I've sometimes had the need to introduce a new concept into an already existing design to help alleviate some constraint that proved too difficult to work with.

Sometimes you just need to break the rules.

That isn't to say that rules …
entry.content:
During my last 3-4 years of my professional life I've sometimes had the need to introduce a new concept into an already existing design to help alleviate some constraint that proved too difficult to work with.

Sometimes you just need to break the rules.

That isn't to say that rules or constraints are completely bad. It's a guidance towards something that has already been atleast somewhat understood, and can help bring a team on the same page.

Have you ever worked on an established product and found the need to "inject" a new concept into the mix?

entry.title: JavaScript fatigue
entry.summary:
Do you suffer from JavaScript fatigue? I know that I do (sometimes.)

I think what JavaScript fatigue describes is the feeling of dread that people get when trying to write modern web applications with modern JavaScript-based technologies.

Why do people get this feeling of dread?

I think it's because people …
entry.content:
Do you suffer from JavaScript fatigue? I know that I do (sometimes.)

I think what JavaScript fatigue describes is the feeling of dread that people get when trying to write modern web applications with modern JavaScript-based technologies.

Why do people get this feeling of dread?

I think it's because people (myself included) get tired of "having" to learn yet another technology which the creators pitch as the "one solution to rule them all."

However I think what underlies this feeling if the fact that people (again myself included) know that you don't need something like React, Vue og Svelte to solve really simple problems, like having a modal appear and disappear when a person activates a button.

I've also gotten really tired with the fact that to do anything modern with JavaScript you have to invest a lot of time into a building toolchain for JavaScript, like Webpack. To me, when using these tools it doesn't feel like I'm solving a problem; it just feels like I'm creating one and setting myself up for failure.

I used to be really excited about new JavaScript frameworks; it seemed like they were all going to solve my problems when developing frontend applications.

Now I usually stick to simple scripts on pages, when I can. In fact I'm trying to pull React out of our Admin application for Relevance.

entry.title: Initial commit
entry.summary:
Welcome to my personal website. Here I plan on spewing out random thoughts in the form of blog posts.

This website is currently hosted on GitHub pages, and is based on the Pelican static site generator.

$ whoami

I'm Niels, a professional software developer. I currently work for Ekstra Bladet as …
entry.content:
Welcome to my personal website. Here I plan on spewing out random thoughts in the form of blog posts.

This website is currently hosted on GitHub pages, and is based on the Pelican static site generator.

$ whoami

I'm Niels, a professional software developer. I currently work for Ekstra Bladet as a Data Engineer.

I've been programming for the better part of my life at this point. I started out wanting to make a video game in Game Maker when I was 10-11. I never finished that game, but I fell into the rabbithole that is programming, web development, software development, etc. So far I've had a lot of fun and I've gained a ton of personal experience with primarily Python and Web Development, and I'm always looking for new and interesting technologies to learn.
